AFTCoop 0.2.4
  • Reference
  • Changelog

    Variable Screening method

    variable_screening.Rd

    This function selects a subset of relevant features from a set of potential features based on their association with patient survival, allowing a preliminary reduction in the number of variables before applying a penalized regression approach.

    The function supports the following ranking methods:

    • absmg: Marginal utilities ordered from largest to smallest (absolute value).

    • mg: Regression coefficients ordered from largest to smallest; both top and bottom perc are selected.

    • mgpadj: Marginal utilities filtered by adjusted p-values less than thresh.

    • mgpval: Variables ranked using \(-\log_{10}(p\text{-value}) \times |\beta|\), combining significance and effect size.

    variable_screening(
  X,
  y,
  delta,
  family = c("cox", "AFT"),
  model = c("weibull", "loglogistic", "lognormal"),
  rank = c("absmg", "mg", "mgpadj", "mgpval"),
  topn = 500,
  perc = 0.1,
  thresh = 0.5
)

    Arguments

    X

    Matrix of covariates of dimension n x p_view, where n is the number of samples and p_view is the number of variables.

    y

    Vector of observed survival or censoring times of length n.

    delta

    Vector of censoring indicators (1 = event, 0 = censored) of length n.

    family

    Character string specifying the survival model. Options are cox or AFT.

    model

    Character string specifying the AFT model. Options are weibull, lognormal, or loglogistic. Ignored if family = cox.

    rank

    Character string specifying the ranking method. Options are absmg, mg, mgpadj, or mgpval.

    topn

    Integer specifying the number of top features to return (default is 500). Used when rank = absmg or rank = mgpval.

    perc

    Numeric value in (0,1) specifying the proportion of top and bottom features to select (default is 0.1). Used when rank = mg.

    thresh

    Numeric threshold for adjusted p-values (default is 0.5). Used when rank = mgpadj.

    Value

    A vector of indices corresponding to the selected features:

    • Top topn features for absmg and mgpval.

    • Top and bottom features for mg.

    • Features with adjusted p-values below thresh for mgpadj.

    Details

    The function performs variable screening based on marginal ranking of features in X, reducing dimensionality while retaining relevant variables.

    • absmg: Selects the top topn features based on absolute marginal utility.

    • mg: Selects both the top and bottom perc proportion of features based on marginal effects.

    • mgpadj: Selects features with adjusted p-values below thresh, using the Benjamini-Hochberg correction (BH or fdr).

    • mgpval: Selects the top topn features ranked by a combined score of p-values and effect sizes.

    Note

    Last updated: 2026-04-22

    See also

    marginal_ranking for computing marginal feature rankings.

    Examples

    if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
set.seed(123)
model <- "weibull"
topn <- 500

data <- generate_data(
  model = model,
  n = 200,
  pu = 150,
  pz = 150,
  tu = 6,
  tz = 6,
  rate = 40,
  sigma_true = 0.5
)

y <- data$times_c
delta <- data$delta

U <- data$U
colnames(U) <- paste0("feature_", 1:ncol(U))

Z <- data$Z
colnames(Z) <- paste0("feature_", 1:ncol(Z))

index_screen_U <- variable_screening(
  U, y, delta,
  family = "AFT",
  model = model,
  rank = "absmg",
  topn = topn
)

index_screen_Z <- variable_screening(
  Z, y, delta,
  family = "AFT",
  model = model,
  rank = "absmg",
  topn = topn
)
} # }

    Contents
